pages.text_excerpt: About | Abena Oppong-Asare MP Facebook Twitter Mail Instagram Youtube Home About Abena Latest news Monthly Newsletters In Parliament Maiden Speech Thamesmead’s Pride in Place Survey Campaign Summer School 2026 In the community Southeastern Campaign Cost of Living Help Next Stop Thamesmead Events Sign up to a ‘Meet your MP’ Coffee Morning with Abena Annual Christmas Card Competition Get in touch Search About Abena Oppong-Asare MP About Me Hi, I’m Abena, your Labour MP for Erith & Thamesmead. I was once told that the chances of someone like me getting a job in Parliament were very slim, and to not even bother trying. The unfairness I felt about this drove me to challenge the barriers I faced, and in fact, motivated me to do all I could to make a positive difference in our society. In 2019 and 2024, I was honoured and privileged to be elected and then re-elected as your Member of Parliament for Erith and Thamesmead. I live right here in our constituency – it’s where my family and friends live and it’s a place I know and understand. Throughout my time as your MP, I have been determined to work with residents, community groups, businesses, and public services to ensure that Erith and Thamesmead gets the fair deal that it deserves. I have fought for better housing, health services and helped secure better transport links. I’m passionate about our community and want to do all I can to make sure everyone who lives here has the opportunity to succeed. However, over 14 long years, successive Conservative Governments failed to deliver for our country, holding back communities like ours. When I spoke to residents across Erith and Thamesmead, I lost count of the number of times people exasperatedly said to me that “nothing in this country works anymore”. This needed to change. That’s why I’m so proud to have campaigned and to have helped elect a Labour Government. Labour was elected on a manifesto of change and to deliver a mission-driven government that will rebuild our economy, our public services and trust in politics. I will continue to work hard to help deliver on these promises and bring these much needed changes to our community. I am also honoured to have been appointed as Parliamentary Secretary in the Cabinet Office. After 5 years of sitting on the Opposition benches, I am now privileged to have the opportunity to deliver real change as a member of the Government to improve the lives of everyone across the country. My website is one of the ways to keep constituents updated about my work and priorities. If you’re interested in hearing more about what I’ve been up to then you can sign up to my monthly newsletters here . Or if you need any support and live in the constituency please don’t hesitate to get in touch . Thank you for reading a bit more about me and what I’ve been doing as your Member of Parliament for Erith and Thamesmead. Yours Sincerely, Abena Oppong-Asare MP Life before Parliament All my life I’ve fought for local people, speaking truth to power on behalf of some of the most vulnerable in our society. Before I was elected as your MP, I have striven to improve living standards for people across Erith and Thamesmead as a resident, a campaigner for local causes and as a local councillor. I represented Erith on Bexley Council, speaking up for our area at the town hall and helping to lead the Labour group of Bexley councillors as Deputy Leader and Spokesperson for Education. I stood up for local people and fought to protect our green spaces from council cuts. I represented the resident’s interests in planning battles to ensure new developments benefitted our community. I also worked closely with residents, the police, and other agencies to address concerns about crime and anti-social behaviour. I worked at the Greater London Authority (GLA), supporting our London Assembly Members to effectively represent the people of London. In the aftermath of the Grenfell tragedy, I led community engagement work for the GLA and the Mayo

pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament | Abena Oppong-Asare MP Facebook Twitter Mail Instagram Youtube Home About Abena Latest news Monthly Newsletters In Parliament Maiden Speech Thamesmead’s Pride in Place Survey Campaign Summer School 2026 In the community Southeastern Campaign Cost of Living Help Next Stop Thamesmead Events Sign up to a ‘Meet your MP’ Coffee Morning with Abena Annual Christmas Card Competition Get in touch Search In Parliament Your voice in Parliament In my maiden speech, which you can watch here , I spoke about my pride in representing our beautiful and diverse community of Erith and Thamesmead in Parliament and highlighted its rich history and exciting future. I also outlined some of the priorities, as communicated to me through conversations with those across the constituency, that I seek to deliver for our area. These priorities include: Bringing the DLR to Thamesmead and improving local transport links Improving local housing and ending homelessness Tackling the threat of climate change and protecting our environment Addressing concerns about crime and anti-social behaviour Fighting to improve our schools and invest in education I’ll be using every opportunity I can to raise these and other issues in Parliament, which I know matter so much to you. You can see my speeches, interventions, and questions in Parliament below and also by using this link here . pages.title: Have Your Say on £20 million Pride in Place Funding for Thamesmead Birchmere Park | Abena Oppong-Asare MP
headings: h2: About Pride in Place Funding
h3: Here for Erith and Thamesmead
h3: Get in Touch
pages.text_excerpt: Have Your Say on £20 million Pride in Place Funding for Thamesmead Birchmere Park | Abena Oppong-Asare MP Facebook Twitter Mail Instagram Youtube Home About Abena Latest news Monthly Newsletters In Parliament Maiden Speech Thamesmead’s Pride in Place Survey Campaign Summer School 2026 In the community Southeastern Campaign Cost of Living Help Next Stop Thamesmead Events Sign up to a ‘Meet your MP’ Coffee Morning with Abena Annual Christmas Card Competition Get in touch Search About Pride in Place Funding I was thrilled to announce a £20 million investment into Thamesmead Birchmere Park as part of the Government’s Pride in Place programme. As the local Member of Parliament for Erith and Thamesmead, I was delighted when I found out that Thamesmead Birchmere Park had been chosen for this investment which will make a real difference to our community after years of being overlooked. Residents know their area better than anyone else. I know they are full of ideas of how they can improve their community. I see this every week in my role as your MP. But what the community needs is the political backing and clear commitment to long term funding to make this happen. I am therefore pleased that an investment of £2 million a year, for the next ten years, is planned, which will allow us to bring about real and long-lasting change in the area and put Thamesmead Birchmere Park on the path to reaching its full potential. Significantly, this investment is about giving power to the community. The views of residents must design the way this money is spent. My job as your MP does not end with securing the funding, I will also play a key role in guaranteeing the money is spent wisely, transparently, and in line with your priorities. I will work with the council, community groups, and local people every step of the way to see that happen. So, the question is what do you think the money should go towards? I’ve launched a survey to capture the ideas of local residents; you can fill it out here or click the button below. This is your chance to think about how we can shape the future of Thamesmead Birchmere Park for the better. I look forward to working with you to make the most of this investment. If you have any questions about this announcement or my survey, then don’t hesitate to reach out to me and my office at abena.oppongasare.mp@parliament.uk Abena joined the Leader of Greenwich Council, Anthony Okereke, to welcome this news. Fill out my Pride in Place Survey here Share Tweet Share Here for Erith and Thamesmead Through my work I am determined to work with local residents, community groups, businesses and public services to ensure that Erith and Thamesmead is put on the map and we get a fair deal for our community. Get in Touch E-mail: abena.oppongasare.mp@parliament.uk Twitter: @abenaopp Facebook: Abena-Oppong-Asare-MP © Copyright - Abena Oppong-Asare MP | Designed by Dumb! pages.title: Campaign Summer School 2026 | Abena Oppong-Asare MP
headings: h3: At my jam-packed 3-day Campaign Summer School, young people aged between 16 and 18 have the opportunity to learn the skills needed to make a real difference in our community.

h3: The Campaign Summer School offers a first-hand insight into the exciting world of political and social campaigning by bringing together the expertise of veteran political activists and partner organisations.
h3: Over the course of 3 days, attendees design their very own local campaigns and get a unique opportunity to turn their ideas into action.
h3: This year, my Campaign Summer School is taking place right here in Erith and Thamesmead from Tuesday 21st July to Thursday 23rd July 2026 .
h3: If you or anyone you know may be interested in taking part, then please find the link to sign up below! The deadline to sign up is Friday 29th May 2026!
h3: If you have any questions, please email my office at: abena.oppongasare.mp@parliament.uk
h2: Sign up using the link below:
h3: – Abena Oppong-Asare MP
h2: Share on Social Media:

h3: In 2025, I hosted my biggest Summer School ever, which welcomed close to 30 attendees. It was a brilliant week with amazing speakers and great feedback from the students. I was also delighted to start my Summer School Alumni Network on LinkedIn following the Summer School to keep all of the attendees connected.

h3: You can find out more about my previous Summer Schools on my website, including a post about my last Summer School in 2025 here . Below, you’ll also find photos and feedback from previous Summer Schools.
h2: Photos:
h2: Feedback:

h3: “Having people from my Council and from nearby area give talks and connect with them opened a big world of opportunities. I realised the power of my voice and listening to all their experiences gives me hope for the future.”
h3: “Loved it, love Abena, keep it up. It’s important for young people to be educated about government and the make up of politics in the country we live in.”
h3: “It was an amazing opportunity, it made it clear what we as young people can do to make a change, it inspired me to start a campaign and campaign to change something I am passionate about”
h3: Here for Erith and Thamesmead
h3: Get in Touch

pages.text_excerpt: Cost of Living help | Abena Oppong-Asare MP Facebook Twitter Mail Instagram Youtube Home About Abena Latest news Monthly Newsletters In Parliament Maiden Speech Thamesmead’s Pride in Place Survey Campaign Summer School 2026 In the community Southeastern Campaign Cost of Living Help Next Stop Thamesmead Events Sign up to a ‘Meet your MP’ Coffee Morning with Abena Annual Christmas Card Competition Get in touch Search Cost of Living help Organisations, agencies and charities that can help with your rising bills. Help with your finances National Debtline provides free, independent debt advice. Phoneline: 0808 808 4000 Entitledto is a benefit checker, to make sure you are getting all the benefits or financial help you are entitled to. Step Change provide help and advice for people who are in debt. Phoneline: 0800 138 1111 Turn2us is a free benefits calculator and information on benefits, grants and other schemes. MoneyHelper and Money Saving Expert offers free advice on guidance on money issues. Help with your finances Debt Free London provides free expert debt advice. Phoneline: 0800 808 5700 Citizens Advice provides free, confidential, impartial advice and support. Greenwich Welfare Rights Service provides advice on benefits, tax credits, and grants Phoneline: 020 8921 6375 Plumstead Law Centre Service provides legal advice and effective advocacy for all welfare benefit appeal matters. Email: info@pclc.org.uk , Phoneline: 020 8853 9993 Help with your energy and bills: Government schemes Government Advice for homeowners to make improvements that could make it cheaper to heat your home. Winter Fuel Payment is an annual payment for heating bills and is available to those born before 25/09/1956. An extra ‘ Cost of Living’ payment is available to those receiving certain benefits and those entitled to a Winter Fuel Payment. Warm Home Discount Scheme is a one-off discount on electricity bills for those on a low income or on the Guarantee Credit element of Pension Credit. Cold Weather Payment is available to those receiving certain benefits or on Support for Mortgage Interest. You’ll get £25 for each 7 day period of very cold weather. Help with your energy and bills Energy Saving Trust provide advice on making your home more energy efficient and lowering your bills. British Gas Energy Trust have free resources, including the Bounce Back Checklist, and grants to remove energy debt, fund emergency fuel credit, and provide boiler replacements. National Energy Action offer free advice on fuel debt, switching suppliers, energy efficiency, and minimising energy bills. Phoneline: 0800 304 7159 LiveWell Greenwich offer advice and support for residents who may be at risk during cold weather. Phoneline: 0800 470 4831 Big London Energy Switch scheme to lower energy costs through collective energy switching. WaterHelp and WaterSure – Thames Water programme to give 50% discount on a household water bills. Available to low income voters. Email: ecs@thameswater.co.uk, Phoneline: 0800 009 3652 Help with food Bexley food bank Free 3-day emergency food supplies. Available to anyone with a food voucher. Avery Hill Christian Fellowship (Tues 10am-12pm, Thur 12-3pm) New Community Church (Mon 11am-1pm, Tues 12:30-3pm) Queen Street Baptist Church (Wed 10-12pm, Sat 10am-12pm) Trinity Baptist Church (Mon 1-3pm, Fri 10:30am-12:30pm) Greenwich food bank Free 3-day emergency food supplies. Available to anyone with a food voucher. East Plumstead Baptist Church (Mon 4:30-6:30pm) Emmanuel Baptist Church (Tues and Thurs 10am-1pm) St Peter’s Catholic Church (Wed 5pm- 7pm) If you are struggling to afford groceries and food, consider joining a local food pantry . It costs £3.50 a week for £20 worth of food, so you can feed your whole family for much less. Email: Londonfoodclubs@family-action.org.uk Greenwich Co-operative Development Agency (GCDA) supports communities to be healthy through training programmes at community hubs. Run at Abbey Wood Community Centre, Glyndon C

pages.text_excerpt: Events | Abena Oppong-Asare MP Facebook Twitter Mail Instagram Youtube Home About Abena Latest news Monthly Newsletters In Parliament Maiden Speech Thamesmead’s Pride in Place Survey Campaign Summer School 2026 In the community Southeastern Campaign Cost of Living Help Next Stop Thamesmead Events Sign up to a ‘Meet your MP’ Coffee Morning with Abena Annual Christmas Card Competition Get in touch Search Sign up for a Coffee Morning with Abena If you are a resident of Erith and Thamesmead and would like to join Abena for a ‘Meet your MP’ Coffee Morning then sign up here Sign Up Book a virtual surgery with Abena If you are a resident of Erith and Thamesmead and need assistance with an issue you can book a meeting with me by email Book a virtual surgery Virtual surgeries If you are a constituent of Erith and Thamesmead and would like to speak to me about a specific issue you are in need of assistance with you can book a virtual advice surgery appointment. My office has been experiencing an extremely high volume of casework relating to Covid-19 so I will be prioritising urgent cases. Please email me at abena.oppongasare.mp@parliament.uk to request a virtual advice surgery appointment. Please include your name, address and, details of the issue you require assistance about. To check if you are a constituent you can search your postcode here: https://www.theyworkforyou.com/ Ask Abena If you have a question relating to policy or a general issue around the constituency you can ask your questions live during one of my live Facebook Q and A events. You can find my first Facebook live here: https://www.facebook.com/AbenaOppongAsareMP/videos/701012944059816/ Details of upcoming events will be posted here and on social media, so keep an eye out. Share this entry Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share by Mail Here for Erith and Thamesmead Through my work I am determined to work with local residents, community groups, businesses and public services to ensure that Erith and Thamesmead is put on the map and we get a fair deal for our community. Get in Touch E-mail: abena.oppongasare.mp@parliament.uk Twitter: @abenaopp Facebook: Abena-Oppong-Asare-MP © Copyright - Abena Oppong-Asare MP | Designed by Dumb! h2: Social Media
h2: Sign-up to receive Monthly Newsletters
h3: Please sign up below to receive monthly e-newsletters, in which you will find updates on local campaigns, my work in Parliament and events taking place near you.
h2: Subscribe

h3: You can contact me about your ideas, issues or concerns in a number of ways. In all correspondence you must ensure you provide your full name, address and telephone number, as I can only deal with constituents who reside in Erith and Thamesmead.
h3: Please review our Privacy statement for more information on how your data will be processed.

pages.text_excerpt: Get in touch | Abena Oppong-Asare MP Facebook Twitter Mail Instagram Youtube Home About Abena Latest news Monthly Newsletters In Parliament Maiden Speech Thamesmead’s Pride in Place Survey Campaign Summer School 2026 In the community Southeastern Campaign Cost of Living Help Next Stop Thamesmead Events Sign up to a ‘Meet your MP’ Coffee Morning with Abena Annual Christmas Card Competition Get in touch Search Get in Touch Member of Parliament E-mail: abena.oppongasare.mp@parliament.uk For security reasons all post for Abena must be sent directly to the parliamentary address. Parliamentary address: Abena Oppong-Asare MP, House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Telephone The office phones are operated Monday to Friday, 10 – 12pm and 2 – 4pm. Casework Queries: 01322 342991 Press & Parliamentary Queries: 0207 219 4892 Social Media Abena is active on social media and you can visit her page on Facebook and Twitter . It is not possible to respond to individual online comments, tweets or direct private messages so you will need to contact the office in the way set out on this page. Sign-up to receive Monthly Newsletters Please sign up below to receive monthly e-newsletters, in which you will find updates on local campaigns, my work in Parliament and events taking place near you. Subscribe * indicates required First Name Last Name Email Address * Postcode e-mail updates I consent to receiving e-mail updates on local news and campaigns from Abena Your details will not be shared with any third parties for marketing purposes and you can unsubscribe at any time. For details please read our privacy policy. Contact Form You can contact me about your ideas, issues or concerns in a number of ways. In all correspondence you must ensure you provide your full name, address and telephone number, as I can only deal with constituents who reside in Erith and Thamesmead. Please review our Privacy statement for more information on how your data will be processed. Contact form: Here for Erith and Thamesmead Through my work I am determined to work with local residents, community groups, businesses and public services to ensure that Erith and Thamesmead is put on the map and we get a fair deal for our community.
